message:
fix(database): cancel-aware result set draining and overflow-safe packet reads

MySQL:
- read_result_set() now accepts a &Cx parameter and checks
  cx.is_cancel_requested() on each row-fetch iteration, returning
  MySqlError::Cancelled with the cancel reason. This prevents
  unbounded reads when a query is cancelled mid-result-set.
- Both call sites (query and execute) pass the cx through.
- PacketReader::read_bytes: use len > data.len().saturating_sub(pos)
  instead of pos + len > data.len() to avoid potential overflow when
  pos is corrupted or near usize::MAX.

Postgres:
- clear_orphaned_transaction() and drain_to_ready() now accept &Cx
  and check cancellation, returning PgError::Cancelled when the
  context is cancelled. drain_to_ready loops are the primary risk
  for blocking indefinitely on a cancelled connection.
- parse_error_and_drain() threads the cx through to drain_to_ready.
- All 12+ call sites updated to pass cx.
- MessageReader::read_bytes: same saturating_sub overflow fix.
- Two test call sites updated to construct a Cx::for_testing().

Pool:
- Remove redundant deadline re-check after sleep in retry loop. The
  loop condition already checks remaining time at the top, and the
  sleep is capped to remaining, so the post-sleep check was dead code
  that could never trigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
